notes: Pickleheads' own Concord city index page headlines '2 Most Popular Pickleball Courts in Concord, CA' and its synthesized summary totals 16 courts (Pine Hollow's 2 + Willow Pass's 14) — but the same /courts/us/california/concord/ URL space actually hosts a THIRD distinct venue detail page, Indoor Pickleball X - Concord (9 indoor courts), which Pickleheads silently excludes from its own city-level aggregate. This is the same pattern already documented in this repo's Pleasanton research (Clubsport of Pleasanton excluded from Pickleheads' own totals despite having a live page) — noted here rather than treated as a discrepancy to resolve. All 3 real venues are included below. Concord has zero existing entries in assets/courts-data.json or assets/venues.json (it's a Phase 2 city per pickleheads-court-research-prompts.md with no cities/concord.html page yet), so every venue here is matchStatus 'new' and notOnPickleheads is empty by definition. IMPORTANT — four apparent 'Concord pickleball' leads were investigated and explicitly ruled OUT as not real/not-in-Concord, so a future pass doesn't waste time rediscovering them: (1) concordpb.com ('Concord Pickleball') describes an 8-court indoor club 'just off I-85' with monthly/yearly passes — I-85 doesn't run anywhere near California; this is Concord, NORTH CAROLINA (matches pickleballconcordnc.com / Cabarrus County sources), a straightforward city-name collision like the Oakland/NJ gotcha this repo's research doc already warns about. (2) 'Finley Center' (4 dedicated + 8 striped pickleball courts, parking off College Avenue) is Finley Community Center in SANTA ROSA, CA (2060 W College Ave), not Concord — confirmed via srcity.org. (3) 'Baldwin Park has public pickleball courts, restrooms, shaded spectator areas' — Concord's real John F. Baldwin Park (2790 Parkside Circle) is confirmed via its official cityofconcord.org facility page to have only a baseball/softball diamond, basketball court, bocce courts, and a softball field; no pickleball. The claim almost certainly conflates it with the separate CITY of Baldwin Park, CA (Los Angeles County), which has its own real Pickleheads/Swimply pickleball listings under the same place name. (4) 'Concord Community Pool has pickleball courts available' (3501 Cowell Rd) — the official city Sports Facilities page lists this address only as Concord Community Park's 7 (unlit) tennis courts plus the separate Concord Community Pool; pickleball is not listed there at all. This looks like a search-summary error blending 'tennis courts here' + 'pool here' into a fabricated 'pool has pickleball courts' claim. (5) 'Clayton Valley Tennis & Pickleball Courts,' 1368 Sumac Circle — appears only on playpickleball.com and a padel-directory mirror of it (thin listings, no court count/hours/fees stated by either); the address itself resolves on Redfin/Zillow/Trulia to an ordinary single-family home (4bd/2ba, sold May 2025), not a court facility. Not on Pickleheads, not on the city's official facilities list, not on Bounce. Treated as a bad geocode in one low-quality directory, not a real venue, and excluded. The official city Sports Facilities page (cityofconcord.org/653/Sports-Facilities) was used as the authoritative cross-check for all of the above — it itemizes exactly one Pickleball Courts entry (Willow Pass, 14 courts) and separately itemizes Tennis Courts at three locations (Concord Community Park 7, Pine Hollow 2, Willow Pass 4), plus Basketball/Bocce/Volleyball/Soccer-Softball/Pool/Disc Golf/Skate Park — nothing else pickleball-related anywhere on it.

venues.name: Willow Pass Community Park
venues.surface: Outdoor asphalt — 14 dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets
venues.weather: Inland East Bay — hot, dry summers (afternoon highs frequently in the 90s°F, sometimes triple digits), mild wet winters, and far less coastal fog or wind than San Francisco or the Peninsula.
venues.notes: The flagship, best-corroborated Concord venue and the ONLY one the city's own dedicated Pickleball page (cityofconcord.org/649/Pickleball) discusses — it's described there as 'one of the largest Pickleball complexes in Northern California.' The 14 pickleball courts are separate from the park's own 4 lighted tennis courts (both are itemized independently on cityofconcord.org/653/Sports-Facilities) — not a double-count of the same asphalt. reservable set to 'Mixed': the official page describes ordinary walk-up/rotation play (non-permit-holders play one game to 11 then vacate when courts are full) alongside a permit requirement for organized groups over 8 players, with reservations run through 'Active.net' / teamsideline.com/concord per the official page's own contact section; bookingUrl points to the city's ActiveCommunities program-search portal (the closest live link found to that reservation system), which surfaces registration/permit-style booking rather than a simple pick-a-court-time-slot page for casual individual play — casual players do not need to book anything. googleMapsUrl's cid was derived by hex-decoding the feature ID (0xa1dfa0febde1b421) embedded in a live 'google.com/maps/place/2748+E+Olivera+Rd...' URL surfaced via search, converted to decimal via a local script; WebFetch on maps.google.com itself is blocked (confirmed HTTP 403, consistent with this repo's other pickleheads-research files), so the resulting pin was not independently re-verified by loading the page. lat/lon (37.987144, -122.021073) was corroborated by two independent search results tied specifically to the 2748 E Olivera Rd address; OpenStreetMap/Nominatim's own 'Willow Pass Community Park' node returned a nearby but distinct point (37.9881683, -122.0215616), plausible for a 40-acre multi-facility park (community center, Pixieland amusement area, softball fields, outdoor fitness area, the courts) where different sources may pin different features within the same park rather than a real location conflict.

venues.name: Indoor Pickleball X - Concord
venues.address: 1695 Willow Pass Rd, Ste 2, Concord, CA 94520
venues.surface: Indoor acrylic/concrete courts, dedicated permanent lines and nets, climate-controlled
venues.price: Bronze membership: $0 to join, pay-as-you-play (open play from $15/hr, court booking from $40/hr). Silver: $129 (waitlist only), ~50% off most services. Gold: $159/month + $129 initiation — unlimited off-peak/peak open play, up to 4 hrs/week of court reservations, free organized play.
venues.skill: Not specified by Pickleheads; the venue's own site describes itself as welcoming to all ages and skill levels
venues.weather: Inland East Bay — hot, dry summers (afternoon highs frequently in the 90s°F, sometimes triple digits), mild wet winters, and far less coastal fog or wind than San Francisco or the Peninsula. (Indoor, climate-controlled facility — outdoor weather doesn't affect play here.)
venues.notes: A membership-based indoor club, not free/walk-up — in scope per this repo's stated standard that membership clubs are included as long as they're described accurately. Pricing reconciled from two overlapping but not identical descriptions found across sources (a Pickleheads-sourced summary citing flat $15/hr open play + $40/hr booking for Bronze visitors, vs. indoorpickleballx.com's own membership page distinguishing off-peak/peak tiers and listing Silver/Gold specifics) — the price field above combines both without inventing a reconciliation neither source stated outright; the venue's own site is treated as authoritative for the membership tier structure. lat/lon and the OSM source above are unusually strong for this venue: OpenStreetMap has a POI literally named 'Indoor Pickleball X' at this address (not just a building-level geocode), independent of Pickleheads. googleMapsUrl's cid was derived the same way as Willow Pass's — hex-decoding a feature ID (0x6d0068d919dc8a50) from a live maps.google.com URL surfaced via search, converted to decimal locally, not re-verified by directly loading the blocked maps.google.com page, and it's possible (though considered unlikely given the OSM corroboration at the same address) that this pin resolves to the shopping center/building rather than the specific business.

venues.name: Pine Hollow Tennis Courts
venues.surface: Outdoor hard court — permanent lines, shared tennis/pickleball striping
venues.netType: Bring your own net (permanent lines, no installed pickleball net)
venues.weather: Inland East Bay — hot, dry summers (afternoon highs frequently in the 90s°F, sometimes triple digits), mild wet winters, and far less coastal fog or wind than San Francisco or the Peninsula.
venues.notes: Real, existing 2-court tennis facility per the official city site (cityofconcord.org/653/Sports-Facilities lists 'Pine Hollow Courts — corner of Pine Hollow Road and Rolling Woods Way — Two lighted courts'), but the city's OWN pickleball-specific page (cityofconcord.org/649/Pickleball) discusses only Willow Pass and never mentions Pine Hollow at all — pickleball usage here is corroborated only by third-party aggregators (Pickleheads, Bounce, HAQ Pickle, globaltennisnetwork.com), all agreeing on 2 outdoor hard courts with permanent lines but no installed pickleball net (bring your own). Marked confirmed:true on the strength of 3+ independent aggregators agreeing with each other and with the city's tennis-facility listing on court count and outdoor/hard-surface, but this official-pickleball-page silence is a real gap a human may want to resolve (e.g. a phone call to confirm the striping is city-sanctioned and not informal). Address discrepancy: aggregators (Pickleheads, Bounce, HAQ Pickle) consistently give a civic address of 5491 Silver Sage Ct, 94521, which is used here, but the official city page and OpenStreetMap both independently describe/pin the courts at the Pine Hollow Road / Rolling Woods Way cross-street instead (OSM has two nearly-identical entries, one tagged to each street) — likely the same small neighborhood-park facility described two different ways (civic mailing address vs. cross-street), not two different locations; lat/lon above uses the tightly-clustered cross-street coordinate (three independent sources within ~30m of each other) rather than a geocode of the Silver Sage Ct address, which was not independently confirmed. lights set to false despite the official facility page saying 'lighted': Pickleheads-hosted player comments describe the lights as non-functional after a copper-wire theft, with the city declining to repair due to the vandalism — a current-conditions report that a formal facilities page is unlikely to reflect quickly; flagging this explicitly rather than silently trusting either source. Comments also note the courts are frequently covered in pine needles from nearby trees (a maintenance/playability note, not a fact about the courts' existence). No numeric Google Maps cid was found for this venue despite several targeted searches (only a hex 'ChIJ'-style place ID or no maps.google.com URL at all turned up) — left null per this repo's cid-only rule rather than falling back to a place_id or /maps/place/...data= URL.
